item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nowhere in the article does the author "stereotype", especially not misogynistically. It's an SCP that involves someone in a specific role, and portrays them as acting in an anomalous way to progress the story. Nowhere in the article does it say "all single mothers are bad and/or hate their kids."

You're allowed to not like an SCP. You're not allowed to purposely make the author feel bad in the process. You're definitely not allowed to make it seem like the author is being a bad person for writing it.1

Footnotes
1. Unless they clearly are, which is far from the truth in this case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
playing into misogynistic stereotypes about single mothers to mine real human trauma for shock content is beneath this wiki

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would like to learn more about what you speak of here. Part of what eludes me as an amateur writer is a better understanding of how to write… "memorably" is the best word I can think of to use, and to do it without leaning on the crutch of using shocking content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This hurts me it's so sad! I really love how you essentially showed the reader how this kid is essentially dying through his writing. Keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Suitably creepy, and I always love to see strange instructions in the Special Containment Procedures. But the logs were predictable and didn't do much for me.

The classifications were also confusing: how is this item Safe (and, by extension, disruption is only Level 2) if it appears on store shelves by seemingly-unknown means? If there is a pattern or a reason this isn't a bigger deal, I don't find it in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like I said in chat this definitely turned my stomach, but you can do that by describing many inhumane acts in detail or from the perspective of the victim. Impact ≠ impression, it just lingers the same way.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The setup with the description was interesting, but the diary logs with the kid totally fell flat. I could guess what was going to happen before it did, and it didn't escalate things or reveal anything unexpected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm also left kind of cold by the (possibly unintended) implication that only mothers might engage in this kind of abuse. The diary also went on for a bit longer than is necessary I think. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea a lot, but the diary leaves me with a feeling of "eh", I felt it could have been written better. Also, the specific "only woman ages 35 to 55 compelled" thing just feels very strange to me. I feel it would be much creepier if any abusive parent could come across these things and get swept up in them.

no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
belonging to one May Jamison

Can't believe I confused that for Mae Jemison.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There has been a lot of things about abusive parents on the wiki lately. Idk why, but it certainly creates some really good articles.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but it's totally FUNNY man come on

item.title: wow
item.content:encoded:
Amazing work !
Reading the card on the bag, i was already thinking "God this one gonna be brutal"
And the diary is just perfect.

Felt bad for a kid => +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Blobber exploded and they made pineapple jello out of his leftovers, which I thought was funny

Truly wonderful the mind of a child is.
why would you do this to Charles Jackton the 2nd? He was such a good boy..

item.title: I promise that I'll get it right.
